chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – Regarding leaving out inviting the people

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/154)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/154)
text: قَالَ لِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِيَّاكُمْ وَ النَّاسَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِذَا أَرَادَ بِعَبْدٍ خَيْراً نَكَتَ فِي قَلْبِهِ نُكْتَةً فَتَرَكَهُ وَ هُوَ يَجُولُ لِذَلِكَ وَ يَطْلُبُهُ

ثُمَّ قَالَ لَوْ أَنَّكُمْ إِذَا كَلَّمْتُمُ النَّاسَ قُلْتُمْ ذَهَبْنَا حَيْثُ ذَهَبَ اللَّهُ وَ اخْتَرْنَا مَنِ اخْتَارَ اللَّهُ وَ اخْتَارَ اللَّهُ مُحَمَّداً وَ اخْتَرْنَا آلَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ عَلَيْهِمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Kuleyb Bin Muawiya Al Saydawi who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘Beware of the people! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, whenever He<sup>azwj</sup> Intends good with a servant, Imprints a spot in his heart, but He<sup>azwj</sup> Neglects him, and he wanders around due to that and seeks it (the true Religion)’.

Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If you all were to speak to the people, say to them, ‘We go where Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Send us, and we choose whatever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Chooses (for us), and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Chose Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> and chose us<sup>asws</sup>, the Progeny<sup>asws</sup> of Muhammad<sup>saww</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Kulayb ibn Mu’awiyah al-Saydawi who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) said to me, ‘Beware of people.’ If Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, wills good for a servant. He places a dot in his heart. He then leaves him alone and for that reason he moves around and searches it (belief).’ The Imam then said, ‘Were you to talk to people, say we have gone where Allah goes, we choose whoever Allah chooses. Allah chose Muhammad and we chose the family of Muhammad, recipients of divine supreme covenant.’”
